laws.title: Public Law 16-114 | Amended Ambassadorial Organization and Reporting Act
laws.chronology_conclusion: Current enactment. The source is intact and operative, and it expressly amends PL 15-102 Sec. III(e); nothing in the reviewed materials repeals PL 16-114.
laws.exact_change: Strike the existing regional-ambassadorship prohibition and insert the new presidential authority, Senate-notice, and Senate-consent language.
source_evidence: SEC II says Section III, Subsection e shall now read the following.
The source text gives the President the only authority to create regional ambassadorships and requires Senate notice and consent.

current_xml_comparison.detail: The branch is only carrying a note in the title 22 area; it does not implement the textual amendment to PL 15-102 Sec. III(e).

final_recommended_action: Apply the Sec. III(e) substitution to PL 15-102.
Replace the boilerplate note with an honest amendment note.
Remove the Trello link.

laws.title: Public Law 16-115 | National Security Threat Mandatory Notification to Congressional Leadership Act
laws.chronology_conclusion: Current enactment. It creates an operative congressional-notification duty and defines the triggering terms; the 18 U.S.C. 1512 reference is a cross-reference, not a textual amendment.
laws.exact_change: Add a title 50 statutory note for the mandatory notification scheme and treat the 18 U.S.C. 1512 reference as cross-reference only.
source_evidence: SEC III assigns a designated Executive Branch individual to notify Congressional Leadership of foreign threats.
SEC IV says failures are classified as a crime under U.S. Code 1512.

current_xml_comparison.detail: The branch note is boilerplate and does not distinguish the 18 U.S.C. 1512 reference as cross-reference only; the Trello link remains.

final_recommended_action: Replace the boilerplate note with an honest statutory note under title 50.
Record the 18 U.S.C. 1512 reference as cross-reference only.
Remove the Trello link.

laws.title: Public Law 16-116 | Combatant Command Re-establishment Act
laws.chronology_conclusion: Current enactment. It repeals the OCJCS Reform Act of 2022 / PL 7-48 and re-establishes six Unified Combatant Commands with reporting and command-and-control rules.
laws.exact_change: Mark PL 7-48 as repealed and add a title 10 note for the re-established combatant-command framework and reporting chain.
source_evidence: The source says the OCJCS Reform Act of 2022 shall be repealed in its entirety.
The source says the following Unified Combatant Commands shall be established in the United States Military.

current_xml_comparison.detail: The branch only carries a note in title 50 and does not apply the repeal-marking for PL 7-48.

final_recommended_action: Apply repeal-marking to the OCJCS Reform Act of 2022 / PL 7-48.
Re-anchor the note toward title 10 combatant-command provisions.
Remove the Trello link.

laws.title: Public Law 16-117 | The Clean Up Act
laws.chronology_conclusion: Current repealing enactment. Its only substantive effect is to repeal Senate Joint Resolution 2 and Senate Joint Resolution 3.
laws.target: Senate Joint Resolution 2; Senate Joint Resolution 3
laws.exact_change: Record the repeal of the two joint resolutions and keep PL 16-117 only as repeal history.
source_evidence: SEC 3 repeals Senate Joint Resolution 2.
SEC 3 repeals Senate Joint Resolution 3.

current_xml_comparison.detail: The branch mislabels a current repealing resolution as repealed or superseded and uses a generic title 50 anchor even though the target is uncodified joint resolutions.

final_recommended_action: Replace the boilerplate note with a concise historical note.
Correct the status to current.
Remove the Trello link.
Exclude PL 16-117 from permanent Code.

laws.title: Public Law 16-118 | The Uniformed Capitol Police Act of 2023
laws.chronology_conclusion: Current enactment. It repeals H.R. 8013 Section III and replaces that section with a new Capitol Police Board structure and powers.
laws.exact_change: Re-anchor to title 2 and replace H.R. 8013 Section III with the new Capitol Police Board membership and powers.
source_evidence: SEC 2 says the listed section of H.R. 8013 shall be repealed in its entirety.
SEC 3 says Section III shall now state the following.

current_xml_comparison.detail: The branch note sits in title 18, but the operative target is Capitol Police governance under title 2, so the repeal and replacement are not implemented.

final_recommended_action: Apply the repeal/substitution to the Capitol Police Board text at the title 2 anchor.
Re-anchor from title 18 to title 2.
Remove the Trello link.

laws.title: Public Law 17-124 | Law Enforcement Accountability Act (LEAA)
laws.chronology_conclusion: Current enactment. It is a free-standing, generally applicable law-enforcement framework with no self-repeal or sunset, and it should not be reduced to a mere note.
laws.exact_change: Create real Code sections for the definitions, canine deployment rules, use-of-force rules, and enforcement provisions instead of keeping the act as a note-only wrapper.
source_evidence: The source contains a definitions section, a canine-deployment section, a use-of-force section, and an enforcement section.
The Act says law enforcement shall only use force that is objectively reasonable.

current_xml_comparison.detail: The branch adds only a boilerplate note at title 18. Because the act is substantive permanent general law, it should be enacted as real Code section(s), not merely a note.

final_recommended_action: Codify as new permanent sections in the appropriate law-enforcement placement rather than a bare note.
Remove the Trello link.
Correct the duplicated SEC5 numbering and the missing SEC6 in editorial notes.
